fields.summary: Checkout service returns 503 / ConnectionPoolTimeout under load
fields.description: During the Tuesday traffic peak the checkout service began returning HTTP 503 with ConnectionPoolTimeout errors. Root cause: the Postgres connection pool was exhausted because a slow query held connections open with no statement timeout, so new requests waited past the pool checkout deadline.

resolution.description: Added statement_timeout=3s on the checkout role and capped the pgbouncer pool_size. Pool exhaustion has not recurred.

fields.summary: Duplicate charge regression in payments under retry
fields.description: Customers were charged twice for a single order. The payments service retried the capture call on a timeout, but the capture was not idempotent, so a duplicate_charge was created when the original request had actually succeeded.

resolution.description: Made payment capture idempotent with a per-order idempotency key; retries now de-duplicate at the provider.
